title: Numbness/tingling
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped adult sensory identity, distribution, onset, progression, associated weakness, bowel or bladder context, sensation, power, reflexes, gait, selected metabolic tests, urgent features, and clinician referral fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.

source.title: Suspected neurological conditions: recognition and referral

source_limitations: People aged 16 years or younger are outside the exact source.

Trauma, skin changes, sensory level, coordination, cranial nerves, temperature sensation, combined B12/folate, imaging, prior notes, generic plans, analgesic plans, safety-netting, and fixed follow-up remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, investigation selection, urgency, treatment, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
